methods.description: A two-way ANOVA was performed to test for association between pollen grain size, number of apertures, and time to 95% rupture using R software.

steps.description: A two-way ANOVA was performed using R software to test for association between pollen grain size and number of apertures and time to 95% rupture.
steps.searchable_summary: perform two-way ANOVA using R to test associations

methods.method_name: Barcode and Primer Pair Performance Evaluation
methods.description: Evaluation of barcode and primer pair performance based on the number of species from the mock community returned from sequencing runs.

steps.description: Barcode and/or primer pair performance were evaluated by the number of species from the mock community returned from sequencing runs.
steps.searchable_summary: evaluate barcode and primer performance by species count from sequencing

steps.description: Correct genus assignment was accepted as indication of species presence.
steps.searchable_summary: verify species presence by correct genus assignment

steps.description: A species was considered present when five or more reads per species were recovered in at least two of the three replicates in one or more exine rupture treatment.
steps.searchable_summary: confirm species presence by read count criteria

methods.description: Evaluation of the effect of exine rupture treatment on read proportions returned, determining optimal exine rupture level for each barcode.

steps.description: In evaluation of the effect of exine rupture treatment on read proportions returned, any presence (i.e. singletons reads and above) of the known species was counted.
steps.searchable_summary: count species presence by read proportions for exine rupture effect

steps.description: The resulting read proportion of a species was subtracted from the expected read proportion and then totaled for each treatment and replicate.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate deviation from expected read proportions

steps.description: The optimal exine rupture level for each barcode was determined to be the treatment that produced the lowest additive value of deviation from expected proportion.
steps.searchable_summary: determine optimal exine rupture level by lowest deviation
